pypsa.notes: Native SCOPF via extra_functionality with LODF-based contingency constraints; 50 contingencies on 10,000-bus network in 30s solver time (A-9, C-8)

powermodels.notes: Manual Benders cutting plane with LODF injection; functional but convergence not demonstrated on feasible instance (A-9)

powersimulations.notes: Manual LODF constraint injection into JuMP model; HiGHS crashes on 10,000-bus MEDIUM network (A-9, C-8)

gridcal.notes: Native LODF-based SCOPF via consider_contingencies parameter; vacuous pass on uncongested 10,000-bus network (A-9, C-8)

pandapower.notes: No SCOPF capability; manual PTDF/LODF construction + external solver required, OOM at 10,000-bus scale (A-9, C-8)

matpower.notes: Benders iteration with LODF constraint injection via mpc.A/l/u; N-1 infeasible on test case + MIPS numerical issues; blocking durability (A-9)

pypsa.notes: Available in PF context but blocked in OPF; custom Linopy constraint needed (A-11)

powermodels.notes: Requires ~150 LOC custom PTDF-based JuMP OPF; no built-in formulation (A-11)

powersimulations.notes: Native distributed slack in power flow via PowerFlows.jl (A-11 pass)

gridcal.notes: Distributed slack hardcoded off in OPF; blocking limitation (A-11)

matpower.notes: Post-processing distributed slack via makePTDF with slack_weights; stable workaround using documented API (A-11)

pypsa.notes: Generator tranche decomposition approximates PWL; no native SOS2 support (P2-2)

powermodels.notes: First-class PWL support via SOS2 constraints across all solver backends (P2-2)

powersimulations.notes: Native SOS2 formulation across all thermal formulations and MIP solvers (P2-2)

gridcal.notes: Only polynomial cost model; MATPOWER PWL costs fitted to quadratic on import (P2-2)

pandapower.notes: Native create_pwl_cost() API; clean and documented (P2-2)

matpower.notes: Native first-class support via gencost matrix with MODEL=1; auxiliary variable LP formulation (P2-2)

pypsa.notes: No PSS/E import method of any kind (P2-1)

powermodels.notes: Built-in parser supports v33 only; v31 fails on header mismatch (P2-1)

powersimulations.notes: PowerSystems.jl PTI parser supports v33/v35 only; v31 absent (P2-1)

gridcal.notes: Parser declares v29-v35 but hardcoded to v35 field layout; v31 fails (P2-1)

pandapower.notes: No PSS/E parser; converter would need 2-4 weeks to build (P2-1)

matpower.notes: Native psse2mpc() supports RAW revisions 24-33+ with auto-detection; also has save2psse() for export (P2-1)

pypsa.notes: extra_functionality callback provides full Linopy model access; 8/8 extensibility tests pass (B-1)

powermodels.notes: Two-level API enables constraint injection in 4 LOC with dual extraction (B-1)

powersimulations.notes: JuMP model access via PSI internals; clean but requires package-internal knowledge (B-1)

gridcal.notes: Monkey-patch PulpLpModel.solve relying on internal constraint naming conventions; fragile (B-1)

pandapower.notes: Monkey-patch PYPOWER internals; fragile, depends on undocumented module references (B-1)

matpower.notes: Documented mpc.A/l/u custom constraint API with dual extraction; stable workaround (B-1)

pypsa.notes: Native SCUC via pypsa.optimize with commitment constraints; SCED via bound manipulation (A-5, A-6)

powermodels.notes: No unit commitment capability; entirely outside the tool's scope (A-5)

powersimulations.notes: Built-in ThermalStandardUnitCommitment with clean formulation hierarchy (A-5, A-6)

gridcal.notes: SCUC via snapshot OPF orchestration; loses inter-temporal coupling at scale (A-5, A-6)

pandapower.notes: No unit commitment; SCED reduces to single-period ED only (A-5, A-6)

matpower.notes: MOST provides built-in SCUC/multi-period/storage with CommitSched output for UC-to-ED transfer; GLPK exit flag bug blocks result extraction (A-5, A-6)
